pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take:
I think Devin means well, but when you look at Haiti squaring off against Morocco, bless his heart, it's like sending a paper airplane into a hurricane. Look, Devin, or "Ghost Pepper" as he's known in the ring, sits just one point shy of the top, but I have to wonder what kind of strategy leads to a Tier 4 team like Haiti facing off against the solid North African contenders. "Bubba G," or Gunner, looks poised to capitalize on this, and I'll say it now: Morocco will have too much for Haiti. You'll see "Ghost Pepper's" standings slip unless an act of divine intervention occurs—though I'm not holding my breath. 

Turning our eyes northwards, Zach, or "Mustard Boy," must be hoping that Canada's hosting vibes will get to them when Switzerland takes the field against the Canadians. The Swiss are solid, organized, and very much the result of Zach's calculated approach to the draft, but you still have to wonder if his matchup selections are more condiment than meat. I think Zach's Switzerland will edge out a win, but let's be honest, the real winner here is the Vancouver tourism board. 

Then there's The Backpass Assassin, Gayden, whose unfortunate standing reflects a, shall we say, creative draft choice in the Korean side. As South Africa lines up against them today in Mexico, you have to hand it to him—he's got Korea Republic in action, at least. But I feel like Gayden is relying on hope rather than strategy, a common theme when sitting at the bottom of the table. Devin might see South Africa as a step to advancement, and I suspect they'll face down and outmuscle The Backpass Assassin's squad in this head-to-head.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Brazil Poised for Victory Against Undrafted Scotland
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach banking on Brazil to lift him from fourth place.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G's Morocco Set to Challenge Ghost Pepper's Haiti
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner likely to pad his lead with Morocco as Devin stays close.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin's Korea Republic Faces Ghost Pepper's South Africa
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden hopes Korea shines brighter than his draft strategy.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Uzbekistan Falls Short in 5-0 Rout by Portugal
pundit_takes.subtitle: Noisemaker's missteps continue as Rafe gains zero points from defeat.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner reigns supreme at the top, but Rafe's starter pack still shocks the world.
Rafe's draft looks like a dartboard—Nigeria, Poland, Jamaica all screamed 'see ya later!'
Rafe and Gayden: a brutal father-son stalemate. Thanksgiving's gonna be awkward.
Sim had Gunner on top—then Rafe joined and the chaos ensued.
